Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 1, 091 + [Sakikku 26][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
oo NaN  (start of obverse missing)
o 1'o 1'

[...] x [...]

(o 1') [...] ... [...]

o 2'2'

[...] DINGIR x x [...]

(o 2') [...] ... [...]

o 3'3'

[...]-⸢šu₂? ŠUB?-šu₂?⸣ [...] ⸢SIG₇⸣ <ŠU> ⸢KI.SIKIL⸣.LIL₂.[LA₂.EN.NA]

(o 3') [...] ... it fell on him [...] yellow-green: hand of the ardat lilî-demon.

o 4'4'

[... i?]-⸢tu₄?⸣ an-nu-u šu₂-u₂ i-qab-bi ⸢be⸣-[en-nu ṣa]-⸢i-du DAB-su uš-te-zib₂?⸣

(o 4') [...] ... he says "it (is) him (again)!": the roving bennu-illness seized him, he will survive.

o 5'5'

[...] ina U₄ 1-KAM 2-šu₂ 3-šu₂ LA₂-šum-ma ina šer₂-[ti] ⸢SA₅⸣ <ina> AN.⸢USAN₂ SIG₇⸣ AN.⸢TA.ŠUB⸣.BA

(o 5') [...] two, three times a day it overwhelms him and, (he is) red in the morning (and) yellow-green in the evening: "falling sickness".

o 6'6'

[... U₄ 1]-⸢KAM⸣ 2-šu₂ 3-šu₂ DAB-su u i-ri-⸢iq⸣ [GID₂-ma] ina ŠUBti TAB.TAB-šu₂

(o 6') [...] two, three times [a day] it seizes him and he becomes green-yellow: [it will be long and] it will continually begin for him in a fall.

o 7'7'

[...] 7-šu₂ uš-ter-di-ma DAB-su uš-te-zeb šum-ma ⸢8⸣-šu₂ <<⸢la₃>> ŠUB⸣-aš-šu₂ ul ⸢uš⸣-te-[zeb]

(o 7') [...] seven times it repeatedly seizes him: he will survive. If, an eighth time, it falls on him: he will not survive.

o 8'8'

[...] ⸢ŠUB⸣-šu₂-ma ana GISKIM-šu₂-ma LA₂-šu₂ na-kid AN.TA.ŠUB.BA ina ma-šal u₄-mi ⸢uš-ta⸣-[qar]-rab-šu₂-ma DUGUD-su

(o 8') [...] it falls on him and it overwhelms him at the expected moment: he is worrying; "falling sickness"; it will come close to him in the middle of the day and it will be burdensome for him.

o 9'9'

[* ŠUB]⸢ti!⸣ ŠUB-su-ma ina U₄ šu₂-a-tu₄ 7-šu₂ uš-ter-di-ma i-ḫi-is-su e-nu-ma um-taš-⸢ši⸣-ru-šu₂

(o 9') [(If)] a fall falls on him and, on that day, seven times, it repeatedly overwhelms him (and) when it has withdrawn, he feels good: hand of a murderer; [he will die].

o 10'10'

⸢i⸣-ṭi-ib ŠU šag-ga-ši [GAM]

o 11'11'

[... ŠUB]ti ŠUB.ŠUB-su IGI-MIN-šu₂ UŠ₂ SA₅-MEŠ : IGI-MIN-šu₂ BADte u DUL[am]

(o 11') [...] a fall has repeatedly fallen on him (and) his eyes fill up with blood : he opens and closes his eyes, his jaw shakes, his hands and his feet stretch out, when the āšipu arrives he ..., he sees who fell on him: hand of the ghost of a murderer.

o 12'12'

⸢ME⸣.ZE₂-šu₂ nu-uš-ša₂ ŠU-MIN-šu₂ u GIR₃-MIN-šu₂ tar-ṣa ina sa-[naq]

o 13'13'

⸢a⸣-ši-pi ʾi-a-bat ša ŠUB-šu₂ ina-ṭal ŠU GU₄ šag-ga-[ši]

o 14'14'

[...] ⸢GIM⸣ ŠUB-⸢šu₂ i⸣-ri-iq-ma ma-gal i-ṣi-iḫ u GIR₃-MIN-šu₂ it-ta-nak-na-an-na : ŠU-MIN-šu₂ GIR₃-MIN-⸢šu₂⸣ ŠU LIL₂.LA₂.⸢EN⸣.[NA]

(o 14') [...] when it has fallen on him he becomes yellow-green and he laughs a lot and his feet are continually contracted : (variant:) his hands and feet: hand of the lilû-demon.

o 15'15'

[...] LA₂-šu₂ LA₂-šum-ma ina KA-šu₂ UḪ₂ DUak AN.TA.ŠUB.BA

(o 15') [...] his fit overwhelms him and saliva flows from his mouth: "falling sickness".

o 16'16'

[...] LA₂-šu₂ LA₂-šum-ma ŠU-MIN-šu₂ u GIR₃-MIN-šu₂ ana ⸢GU₂⸣-šu₂ ik-tap-⸢pa⸣ AN.TA.ŠUB.BA

(o 16') [...] his fit overwhelms him and his hand and his feet curve towards his neck: "falling sickness".

o 17'17'

[... a]-šiš-ti₄ DAB-su UḪ₂ ina KA-⸢šu₂⸣ DUak ⸢ŠUD₃ AD⸣-šu₂ DAB-su GAM

(o 17') [...] a depression seizes him, saliva flows from his mouth: a vow of his father seized him, he will die.

o 18'18'

[...] ⸢LA₂⸣-šu₂ TA iṣ-ṣab-tu-šu UḪ₂ ina KA-šu₂ DUak ŠU LIL₂.LA₂.EN.NA

(o 18') [...] it has overwhelmed him, after it has seized him, his saliva flows from his mouth: hand of the lilû-demon.

o 19'19'

* U₄ ⸢LA₂⸣-šu₂ UB.NIGIN₂.NA-šu₂ i-šaḫ-ḫu-ḫa ŠA₃-šu₂ DAB.DAB-su [ŠA₃]-MEŠ-šu₂ SI.SA₂-MEŠ-šu₂ ŠU GU₄

(o 19') (If) when it has overwhelmed him, his limbs are loosened, his intestines seize him repeatedly (and) he defecates: hand of the ghost.

o 20'20'

* ⸢U₄ LA₂⸣-šu₂ UB.NIGIN₂.NA-šu₂ i-šam-ma-ma-šu₂ IGI-MEŠ-šu₂ NIGIN-⸢MEŠ?⸣[du ŠA₃-šu₂ i]-šaḫ-ḫu-uḫ u mim-ma [ša₂ ana] KA-šu₂ GAR⸢ar₂⸣

(o 20') (If), when it has overwhelmed him, his limbs become paralysed, his face continually twitches, [his intestines] are loosened and whatever he adds [in] his mouth is always, on that day, thrown out from the anus: [hand of a ghost] who died through murder.

o 21'21'

<<*>> ⸢ina⸣ U₄-šu₂ ma-ti-ma ina DUR₂-šu₂ ⸢na⸣-[di?-šu₂? ŠU GU₄] ⸢ša₂?⸣ [ina?] šag-gaš-ti GAM

o 22'22'

[...] LA₂-šu₂ UB.NIGIN₂.NA-šu₂ i-šam-ma-ma-šu₂ i-re-⸢eḫ-ḫe⸣-šum-ma NI₂-šu₂ i-maš-ši U₄ ir-te-ḫu-šu₂

(o 22') [...] it has overwhelmed him, his limbs become paralysed, (unconsciousness) pours over him and he forgets himself; when it has poured on him, his eyes ..., his face becomes red : (variant:) curves, his veins pulsate and he cries out, the tips of his fingers and toes are cold. The āšipu makes this patient speak and he says what he has been made to speak (and), when it has released him, he does not remember what he said: hand of the lilû-demon of laʾbu-disease.

o 23'23'

IGI-MIN-šu₂ SAR x ⸢IGI⸣-MEŠ-šu₂ SA₅ : siḫ-ḫu-⸢ru SA⸣-MEŠ-šu₂ ZI-MEŠ u i-leb-bu ap-pat U-MEŠ

o 24'24'

ŠU-MIN-šu₂ u GIR₃-MIN-šu₂ ⸢ka-ṣa⸣-a GIG BI MAŠ.MAŠ! u₂!-šad-bab-šu₂-ma ša₂ u₂-šad-ba-bu-šu₂ i-qab-bi

o 25'25'

U₄! un-deš-ši-⸢ru⸣-šu ⸢ša⸣ id-bu-bu ⸢la₃⸣ ZU ⸢ŠU LIL₂.LA₂.EN⸣.NA la-aʾ-bi

o 26'26'

[...] ⸢U₄⸣ LA₂-šu₂ ta-lam-⸢ma⸣-šu ⸢DUGUD-su⸣ u u₂-⸢zaq-qat-su⸣ EGIRnu

(o 26') [...] when it has overwhelmed him his torso becomes burdensome and causes him stinging pain, later, it overwhelms him (again) and he forgets himself: "falling sickness"; it will be burdensome for him in the middle of the day.

o 27'27'

⸢LA₂-šum-ma⸣ [NI₂]-šu₂ i-⸢maš-ši⸣ AN.TA.ŠUB.BA [ina] ma-šal u₄-mi DUGUD-su

o 28'28'

[... LA₂]-⸢šu₂ SAG⸣.[KI]-⸢MIN-šu₂ GU₇-MEŠ⸣-šu₂ ŠA₃-šu₂ GAZ-MEŠ-šu₂ ⸢EGIR⸣nu ŠU-MIN-šu₂ u GIR₃-MIN-šu₂ u₂-kap-⸢par₂⸣

(o 28') [... it has overwhelmed] him his temples hurt him, he is in a state of melancholy, later, he rubs his hands and feet, he does not have [...] : (variant:) he rolls, fall : (variant:) hamītu-disease: hand of Ištar; it will leave.

o 29'29'

[...] la₃ TUKU : it-⸢ta⸣-naq-ra-ar₂ ŠUBtu : ḫa-⸢me⸣-tu₄ ŠU d15 ZI⸢be₂?⸣

o 30'30'

[...] LA₂-⸢šu₂ ŠU⸣-[MIN-šu₂] ⸢IGI!⸣.BARas BABBAR ⸢IGI⸣-MIN-šu₂ BALut u UŠ₂ ina KA-šu₂ DUku ana ⸢MUNUS⸣ LIL₂.LA₂.EN.NA ana ⸢NITA⸣ SAL.LIL₂.LA₂.EN.NA

(o 30') [...] it has overwhelmed him, he gazes at his hands, the whites of his eyes roll and blood flows in his mouth: the lilû-demon for a woman, the lilītu-demon for a man.

o 31'31'

[*] ⸢DAB⸣-su ⸢ina AN.USAN₂ DAB⸣.DAB-su ⸢DAB GU₄⸣

(o 31') [(If)] his seizure afflicts him in the evening: seizure of a ghost.

o 32'32'

KI.MIN <(* DAB-su ina AN.USAN₂ DAB.DAB-su)> SAG ŠA₃-šu₂ MU₂-MEŠ GIR₃-MIN-šu₂ ⸢IL₂⸣a ⸢DAB GU₄⸣

(o 32') Ditto ((If) his seizure afflicts him in the evening), the top of his belly is continually swollen, his feet are raised: seizure of a ghost.

o 33'33'

KI.MIN <(* DAB-su ina AN.USAN₂ DAB.DAB-su)> ŠA₃-MEŠ-šu₂ MU₂-ME EN ⸢EN⸣.NUN.MURU₂.BA id-⸢lip⸣ DAB GU₄

(o 33') Ditto ((If) his seizure afflicts him in the evening), his intestines are swollen, he is sleepless until the middle watch: seizure of a ghost.

o 34'34'

KI.MIN <(* DAB-su ina AN.USAN₂ DAB.DAB-su)> IGI-MIN-šu₂ ur-ru-⸢pa⸣ GEŠTU-⸢MIN⸣-šu₂ GU₃.DE₂-MEŠ ⸢DAB⸣ GU₄

(o 34') Ditto ((If) his seizure afflicts him in the evening), his eyes are clouded, his ears continually ring: seizure of a ghost.

o 35'35'

KI.MIN <(* DAB-su ina AN.USAN₂ DAB.DAB-su)> ⸢GEŠTU⸣-MIN-šu₂ GU₃.⸢DE₂-MEŠ⸣ DAB GU₄

(o 35') Ditto ((If) his seizure afflicts him in the evening), his ears continually ring: seizure of a ghost.

o 36'36'

KI.MIN <(* DAB-su ina AN.USAN₂ DAB.DAB-su)> [GEŠTU]-MIN i-⸢šam⸣-[ma]-⸢ma-šu₂⸣ DAB GU₄

(o 36') Ditto ((If) his seizure afflicts him in the evening), (his) ears cause pain: seizure of a ghost.

o 37'37'

[...] ⸢SAG⸣.KI-šu₂ [GU₇]-⸢šu₂⸣ DAB ⸢GU₄⸣

(o 37') [...] his forehead hurts him: seizure of a ghost.

o 38'38'

[...] ⸢TA EN⸣.NUN.AN.[USAN₂] EN EN.⸢NUN⸣.MURU₂.⸢BA id-da⸣-lip-šu₂ ⸢DAB⸣ [...]

(o 38') [...] he has been sleepless from the evening watch until the middle watch: seizure of [...].

o 39'39'

KI.MIN <(* DAB-su ina AN.USAN₂ DAB.DAB-su)> [... an]-nu-u₂ ⸢šu-u₂⸣ [i-qab]-bi ⸢ŠU⸣ [KI].SIKIL.LIL₂.[LA₂.EN.NA]

(o 39') Ditto, [...] he says "it is him (again)!": hand of the ardat lilî-demon.

o 40'40'

[... ša₂] DAB DAB.DAB-su [...] ⸢IGI⸣-MEŠ-[šu₂] ⸢SA₅⸣ u ⸢SIG₇ ŠU⸣ [KI].⸢SIKIL.LIL₂⸣.[LA₂.EN.NA]

(o 40') [...] that-of-seizing afflicts him repeatedly, [...] his face is red and yellow-green: hand of the ardat lilî-demon.

o 41'41'

[...] ⸢ša₂⸣ DAB DAB.DAB-su ⸢U₄⸣ [... ŠU]-⸢MIN⸣-šu₂ u ⸢GIR₃-MIN-šu₂ SIG₇⸣ [ŠU LIL₂].LA₂.EN.⸢NA⸣

(o 41') [...] that-of-seizing afflicts him repeatedly, when [it has affected him] his hands and feet become red-yellow: hand of the lilû-demon.

o 42'42'

[... ša₂] DAB DAB.DAB-su U₄ DAB-[šu₂ ...] ⸢IGI⸣-ME-šu₂ u₂-maš-šad [...] ⸢DUGUD⸣-su KI.MIN MAŠKIM ID₂ [...]

(o 42') [...] that-of-seizing afflicts him repeatedly, when it has affected [him] he rubs [...] his face [...]: it will be burdensome for him; ditto the lurker-demon of the river [...].

o 43'43'

[... ša₂] ⸢DAB-šu₂⸣ šu-te-eq-[ru-ub] U₄ ⸢LA₂⸣-šu₂ ŠU-MIN-[šu₂ ... i]-⸢ḫe-es-si⸣ GIR₃-MIN-šu₂ LA₂⸢aṣ⸣

(o 43') [...] that-of-his-seizing comes close (to him), when it has overwhelmed him, he wrings his hands [...], he stretches out his feet, he trembles a lot and he is tired, he looks at his seizer: hand of the binder-demon.

o 44'44'

⸢ma⸣-gal [ḪULUḪut]-⸢ma⸣ ina-⸢aḫ⸣ [DAB]-⸢su IGI.BARas⸣ ŠU eʾ-e-⸢li⸣

o 45'45'

[... ina EN].NUN.MURU₂.BA [ša₂?] DAB ⸢DAB⸣.DAB-su U₄ ⸢DAB⸣-šu₂ ŠU-MIN-šu₂ u [GIR₃-MIN]-šu₂ SEDa ma-gal u₂-te-⸢te⸣-[eṭ-ṭe]

(o 45') [If during] the middle watch, [that]-of-seizing afflicts him repeatedly, when it has affected him his hands and his [feet] are cold, he is continually much darkened, he repeatedly open [...], his face is red and yellow-green, he continually throws down the hips: hand of the arad lilî-demon; it will be long and he will die [...] the lilû-demon for a woman, the lilītu-demon for a man.

o 46'46'

BAD.BADte ⸢IGI⸣-MEŠ-šu₂ SA₅ u SIG₇ MURU₂-MEŠ ŠUB.ŠUBdi ŠU KI.SIKIL.LIL₂.[LA₂.EN].NA

o 47'47'

[GID₂].DA GAM [...] ⸢ana MUNUS LIL₂⸣.LA₂.EN.NA ana NITA SAL.LIL₂.⸢LA₂.EN⸣.NA

o 48'48'

[... ina ṣal-lu]-⸢ti⸣-šu₂ DAB-su-⸢ma⸣ [DAB-su IGI.BAR]as i-⸢re-eḫ⸣-ḫe-šum-[ma] ⸢NI₂⸣-šu₂ i-maš-ši ⸢GIM ša₂⸣ [...] MUDud

(o 48') [...] it affects him [in] his sleep and he looks at [his seizer], (unconsciousness) pours over him [and] he forgets himself, as [...] he is terrified, he will continually get up [...] criminal [...] the lilû-demon for a woman; he will continually get up.

o 49'49'

[ZI.ZI]⸢be₂⸣ [... sar]-⸢ru⸣ ana MUNUS LIL₂.LA₂.⸢EN⸣.[NA] ZI.ZIbe₂

o 50'50'

[...] ⸢SAG.KI⸣-šu₂ IGI-MIN-⸢šu₂ i⸣-[se-ʾa ...] GU₇-šu₂ ŠU ⸢GU₄⸣

(o 50') [...] his forehead (and) eyes are pressed down [...] hurts him: hand of a ghost.

o 51'51'

[...] x [...] ⸢GU₇⸣-MEŠ-šu₂ ba-mat-⸢su⸣ [...]

(o 51') [...] ... [...] hurt him, his chest [...]

o 52'52'

[...] x

(o 52') [...] ...

o 53'53'

[...] x

(o 53') [...] ...

Reverse
r 1r 1

[...] x [...] x [...]

(r 1) [...] ... [...] ... [...]

r 22

[...] ⸢GU₄⸣.U₄-MEŠ [...] x x ⸢ne?-ḫu ŠU-MIN⸣-šu₂ u GIR₃-MIN-šu₂ [...]

(r 2) [...] jump [...] ... calm, his hands and feet [...]

r 33

[... SAG].⸢KI⸣-MEŠ-⸢šu₂ i⸣-[tar]-⸢ru⸣-ra x x ⸢ŠUB?di?⸣ DA ina ta-ra-ni x [...]

(r 3) [...] his temples are pulsating ... [...]

r 44

[...] ša₂ TUKU-ma i-ra-ʾu-⸢ub EN ŠUB?⸣-šu₂ [...] ḪA RU DAB-su ina x [...]

(r 4) [...] ... and shakes until it seizes him [...] ... his seizure in ... [...]

r 55

[...] x-šu₂ GU₇-MEŠ-šu₂-ma la ⸢i-ṣal⸣-lal ŠU ⸢GU₄⸣

(r 5) [...] his ... continually hurt(s) him so that he does not sleep: hand of a ghost.

r 66

[...] ⸢GIM⸣ aš₂-bu-ma IGI!-[šu₂ i]-⸢ṣa⸣-par ⸢NUNDUN⸣-su ip-paṭ-⸢ṭar⸣ [...] ina KA-⸢šu₂ DU⸣ak ŠU-su GIR₃-šu₂ ta-lam-⸢ma⸣-[šu₂]

(r 6) [...] when he is sitting his eyes wink, his lip is loosened, [...] flows from his mouth, his hand, foot and torso [on] the left side convulse like a slaughtered sheep: "falling sickness". If, when it seized [him ...].

r 77

[ša₂ 2].⸢30⸣ GIM UDU.NITA ṭa-[ab-ḫi] i-nap-⸢pa-aṣ AN⸣.[TA.ŠUB.BA šum]-ma U₄ DAB-[šu₂ ...]

r 88

[... DAB]-šu₂ lib₃-bi lib₃-bi ⸢GU₃⸣.[GU₃si] ⸢IGI⸣-MIN-šu₂ ⸢BADte⸣ [u] ⸢DUL⸣[am KUM₂] ⸢li-iʾ-ba⸣ i-[šu ...]

(r 8) [... it has affected] him he continually shouts "my belly, my belly!", he opens [and] closes his eyes: he has the liʾbu-fever [...] the tips of his fingers and toes are cold, you make this patient speak [...] the lilû-demon.

r 99

[... ŠU]-⸢MIN⸣-šu₂ ⸢u GIR₃-MIN-šu₂ SEDa⸣ GIG šu₂-a-⸢tu⸣ tu-šad-bab-⸢šu₂⸣ [... ŠU LIL₂.LA₂.EN].NA

r 1010

[...] ⸢DAB⸣-šu₂ lib₃-<bi> lib₃-bi GU₃.GU₃si IGI-MIN-šu₂ BADte u [DULam] ⸢KUM₂ li⸣-[iʾ-ba ... ap]-⸢pat?⸣

(r 10) [...] it has affected him, he continually shouts "my belly, my belly!", he opens and [closes] his eyes, he [has] the liʾbu-fever [...] the tips of his fingers and toes become cold, he sees the illness that seized him, he talks to himself [and] he repeatedly changes himself: hand of the lilû-demon of laʾbu-disease.

r 1111

[ŠU].⸢SI!⸣-MEŠ ŠU-MIN-šu₂ u GIR₃-MIN-šu₂ SED-MEŠ GIG <<šu₂>> DAB-šu₂ ina-⸢ṭal KI-šu₂ DU₁₁⸣.[DU₁₁ub u NI₂-šu₂ ut-ta-na]-kar

r 1212

⸢ŠU LIL₂⸣.LA₂.EN.NA [la-aʾ]-bi

r 1313

[...] ⸢U₃⸣.DI i-re-eḫ-ḫu-šu₂ U-MEŠ ŠU-MIN-šu₂ u GIR₃-šu₂ i-⸢na⸣-[pa-ṣa]-ni

(r 13) [...] sleep pours on him, his fingers and toes kick: the "falling sickness" [seized] him in a waste field or a corner.

r 1414

[AN.TA].⸢ŠUB.BA⸣ ina ni-du-ti KI.MIN ina tub₂-qi₂ [DAB]-su

r 1515

[... i-tar]-⸢ru⸣-ra i-ṭa-ma-a u IGI-MEŠ ⸢NIGIN⸣-MEŠdu AN.TA.ŠUB.BA ina ma-šal u₄-⸢mi⸣ [DUGUD]-⸢su⸣

(r 15) [...] tremble, make circulare movements and his face twitches: "falling sickness"; it will be burdensome for him in the middle of the day.

r 1616

[... u₂]-⸢zaq⸣-qa-ta-šu₂ u ⸢IGI-MEŠ⸣-[šu₂ NIGIN]-⸢MEŠdu⸣ AN.TA.ŠUB.BA ina ma-šal u₄-[mi] DUGUD-su

(r 16) [...] cause stinging pain to him and his face twitches: "falling sickness", it will be burdensome for him in the middle of the day.

r 1717

[... IGI]-⸢MIN⸣-šu₂ ⸢BAD⸣-MEŠ [...] ⸢DU₁₁⸣.DU₁₁ub u NI₂-šu₂ ut-ta-na-kar ŠU LIL₂.LA₂.EN.NA

(r 17) [...] he repeatedly opens his eyes [...] he speaks and repeatedly changes himself: hand of the lilû-demon [...] of his god.

r 1818

[...] ša₂ DINGIR-šu₂

r 1919

[... ne]-ḫa-šu₂ KA-šu₂ DAB₅-⸢ma⸣ la₃ ⸢DU₁₁.DU₁₁ub⸣ ŠU GU₄ šag-ga-ši : ŠU GU₄ ⸢qa-li-i⸣

(r 19) [...] are calm, his mouth is affected and he cannot speak; hand of a ghost of a murderer : hand of the ghost of a burnt (person).

r 2020

[... ša₂ bal]-⸢ṭi ne⸣-ḫa-ma i-qal u ⸢mim⸣-ma la i-lem ŠU GU₄ šag-ga-ši : qa-li-⸢i⸣

(r 20) [...] a healthy person are calm and silent but he does not consume anything: hand of the ghost of a murderer : of a burnt (person).

r 2121

[...] GU₃.⸢GU₃si⸣ i-leb-bu ⸢UḪ₂ ina KA⸣-šu ⸢DUak⸣ u GU₂-[su ana] ⸢2⸣.30 zi-ir AN.TA.ŠUB.⸢BA⸣

(r 21) [...] he continually shouts, he cries out, saliva flows from his mouth and his neck is twisted to the left: "falling sickness".

r 2222

[... i-te-ner]-ru-ub TUG₂-⸢su⸣ ŠUB.⸢ŠUB⸣[di iš]-ta-na-su ma-gal [...] ⸢u! KAŠ⸣ la₃ ⸢GUR-ma⸣ [...]

(r 22) [...] he repeatedly puts on (and) throws off his garment, he strongly ... [...] and he no longer [...] beer [...] he does [not] sleep: hand of Ištar, he will recover.

r 2323

[... la₃] i-ṣal-lal ŠU d15? ⸢TI?⸣

r 2424

[...] ⸢IGI-MIN⸣-šu₂ u₂-zaq-qap ⸢SIG₃⸣-su ina SAG-[šu₂] [GUB]⸢az⸣ GAM

(r 24) [...] he makes his eyes protrude: his beater stands in his head, he will die.

r 2525

[... it-ta]-⸢nar⸣-ra-ar₂ IGI-MIN-šu₂ u₂-zaq-qap SIG₃-su ina SAG-šu₂ GUB-ma GAM

(r 25) [If] he continually trembles, he makes his eyes protrude: his beater stands in his head and he will die.

r 2626

[... it]-⸢te⸣-ne₂-et-be₂ ma-⸢gal DU₁₁⸣.DU₁₁[ub u ig]-⸢da-na⸣-al-lu-⸢ut⸣ [...] x ŠU ana NITA SAL.LIL₂.LA₂.EN.NA ZI.ZIbe₂

(r 26) [...] continouously cause pain, he speaks a lot and trembles [...] ...: the lilītu-demon for a man; it will leave.

r 2727

[...] ana ⸢GIG-šu₂ MUD⸣.[MUD]ud u GIŠ.⸢KUN?⸣-MEŠ-⸢šu₂⸣ [...] ⸢u₃ KI.SIKIL⸣.LIL₂.LA₂.EN.[NA] x TAG-ma be-en-nu

(r 27) (If) he is continually frightened by his disease and his loins [...] and the ardat lilî-demon ... he is touched and bennu-illness (gloss: he continually trembles in his bed).

r 2828

: ina KI.NU₂-šu₂ ⸢LUḪ⸣.[LUḪ]

r 2929

* MUD.MUDud ŠU dur-bi-li-ti

(r 29) (If) he is continually afraid: hand of Ištar of Arbela.

r 3030

* ik-kil-lu GU₃.GU₃-šu₂ u šu-[u₂] i-ta-nap-pal-šu₂ U₄ GU₃.GU₃-šu ⸢at⸣-tu₂ man-nu DU₁₁.GA mu-ut-til!-lu ⸢MUŠEN TAG-ma⸣ KI-šu₂ ⸢KEŠDA⸣

(r 30) (If) a lamentation shouts at him and he always answers it (and) when he shouts he says: "who are you?": he is touched by a muttellu-bird and bound to it, it stands in his head; he will die.

r 3131

ina SAG-[šu₂] GUBaz GAM

r 3232

* ik-kil-lu GU₃.GU₃-šu₂ u šu-u₂ ⸢i-ta⸣-nap-pal-šu₂ ZIbe₂ u DU₁₀.GAM!is MAŠKIM ḫar-ba-ti DAB-su

(r 32) (If) a lamentation shouts at him and he always answers it, he stands up and kneels: the demon of desolation seized him.

r 3333

* ik-kil-lu GU₃.GU₃-šu₂ ⸢U₄⸣ GU₃.GU₃-šu₂ ⸢šu⸣-[u₂ i]-ta-nap-pal-šu₂ it-ta-na-as-pak ZI-ma i-kaš₂-šu₂-uš

(r 33) (If) a lamentation shouts at him, when it shouts at him he always answers it, he writhes, he stands up and he is dizzy, he stands up and kneels: his illness will leave him; a roving demon seized him.

r 3434

ZIbe₂ u DU₁₀.GAM! GIG-⸢su⸣ [uš]-te-zeb MAŠKIM mur-tap-pi-du DAB-su

(catchline)
r 3535

* mi-šit-ti pa-ni ma-šid ⸢ta-lam⸣-ma-šu₂ i-šam-ma-am-šu₂ KIN mi-šit-ti

(r 35) "(If) (a person) is struck by facial stroke (and) it paralyses his torso: affliction by stroke",

(1 line blank)
(colophon)
r 3636

⸢59⸣-AM₃ MU ŠID-BI.⸢IM⸣ DUB 1-KAM₂ * ŠUBtu ŠUB-su-ma

(r 36) 59 lines, tablet 1: "(If) a fall fell on him".

(1 line blank)
r 3737

[DUB 25-KAM₂ U₄] ana E₂ GIG KA.PIRIG₃ DU⸢ku⸣ SA.⸢GIG⸣

(r 37) [Tablet 25 "When] an āšipu comes to a patient's house", Ailment.

r 3838

[...] x [...]-⸢ma⸣ BA.AN.E₃

(r 38) [...] ... [...] and checked

r 3939

[...] x-URU₄eš

(r 39) [...] ...-ereš

r 4040

[...] BAN₃.DA

(r 40) [...] young [...].

(end reverse missing)
